description: Bundle Octave on Linux from Ubuntu .deb packages with fonts and an LD_PRELOAD font redirect shim so plotting works out of the box without the user installing anything.

userStories.title: Download and extract Octave .deb packages for Linux
userStories.description: As a developer running download-octave.js --platform linux, I want the script to download Octave 8.4 .deb packages from the Ubuntu archive and extract them to resources/octave/, so Linux has a bundled Octave.
acceptanceCriteria: download-octave.js --platform linux downloads octave_8.4.0-1build4_amd64.deb, octave-common_8.4.0-1build4_all.deb, and liboctave-dev_8.4.0-1build4_amd64.deb from http://archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/pool/universe/o/octave/
Also downloads fonts-freefont-otf_20211204+svn4273-2_all.deb from http://archive.ubuntu.com/ubuntu/pool/main/f/fonts-freefont/
Extracts all packages to resources/octave/ using dpkg-deb -x (or ar x + tar xf as fallback)
resources/octave/usr/bin/octave-cli exists after extraction
resources/octave/usr/share/fonts/opentype/freefont/FreeSans.otf exists after extraction
Running the script twice does not re-download (checks for existing binary)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Ubuntu Noble 24.04 package URLs. The .deb extraction uses dpkg-deb on Debian/Ubuntu systems. For fallback on non-Debian: ar x pkg.deb && tar xf data.tar.* Both approaches must be tried.

userStories.title: Compile font_redirect.so LD_PRELOAD shim and create wrapper script
userStories.description: As part of the Linux bundling, compile a tiny C shared library that redirects font openat() calls from the hardcoded system path to the bundle, and create a wrapper shell script that sets up the environment.
acceptanceCriteria: download-octave.js writes a C source file, compiles it with gcc -shared -fPIC -o resources/octave/font_redirect.so font_redirect.c -ldl, then deletes the source
The shim intercepts openat() calls where pathname starts with /usr/share/fonts/opentype/freefont/ and replaces the prefix with $MATSLOP_OCTAVE_ROOT/usr/share/fonts/opentype/freefont/
The redirect path is read from MATSLOP_OCTAVE_ROOT env var at runtime (not hardcoded)
If gcc is not available, print a warning and skip (plotting won't work but app still runs)

Creates a wrapper script at resources/octave/bin/octave-cli that sets LD_LIBRARY_PATH, LD_PRELOAD (font_redirect.so), MATSLOP_OCTAVE_ROOT, OCTAVE_HOME, GNUTERM=pngcairo, FONTCONFIG_FILE, and execs the real binary at usr/bin/octave-cli
Creates a fonts.conf XML file at resources/octave/fonts.conf that includes both the bundled font dir and /usr/share/fonts as fallback
The wrapper script is executable (chmod +x)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The C shim source should be a string constant in download-octave.js. The wrapper script creates a bin/ directory at resources/octave/bin/ (different from usr/bin/). MATSLOP_OCTAVE_ROOT should point to the resources/octave/ directory (the root of the extracted tree). The real octave binary is at resources/octave/usr/bin/octave-cli.

userStories.title: Update OctaveProcessManager to use -dpngcairo on Linux
userStories.description: When spawning Octave on Linux, ensure the gnuplot graphics toolkit uses pngcairo terminal since the extracted Octave's gnuplot only has cairo-based terminals.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/main/octaveProcess.ts, the init script adds setenv('GNUTERM', 'pngcairo') when process.platform === 'linux'
The capture script in src/renderer/App.tsx uses -dpngcairo instead of -dpng for print() calls when on Linux, OR the GNUTERM env var handles this automatically (test both)
plot(1:10) followed by print('/tmp/test.png', '-dpng') produces a valid non-empty PNG file on Linux
Typecheck passes
All tests pass
userStories.notes: The GNUTERM=pngcairo env var in the wrapper script may be sufficient — gnuplot maps -dpng to pngcairo when GNUTERM is set. Test whether the init-script setenv is also needed or redundant. The capture script uses print(handle, path, '-dpng', '-r150') — verify this produces a real image.

userStories.title: Verify end-to-end plotting in the app
userStories.description: Verify that 2D and 3D plots render in MatSlop using the bundled Octave on Linux.
acceptanceCriteria: Run plot(1:10) via octaveExecute — the print command produces a valid PNG (non-empty, >1KB)
Run surf(peaks) — produces a valid 3D surface PNG
Run title('Hello'); xlabel('X'); ylabel('Y') — labels appear in the PNG
The matslop_export_fig function is loadable by Octave (which matslop_export_fig returns a path)
The capture script in App.tsx detects figures and the Figure panel shows the plot image
Typecheck passes
All tests pass
userStories.notes: Use the bundled Octave at resources/octave/bin/octave-cli. Update ~/.config/matslop/config.json to point to it. The existing capture script runs matslop_export_fig after each command to detect figures. If Plotly JSON export doesn't work with gnuplot, the PNG fallback path (capture script reads the PNG and shows it as an image) should still work.
